question_text: A student heats ice until it becomes steam. Identify the physical changes that occur and explain what remains constant throughout these changes.
correct_answer: Ice melts to water (melting) and water evaporates to steam (evaporation). The substance remains water (H₂O) throughout; only the state (solid to liquid to gas) and the arrangement of particles change
explanation: These are phase changes—physical changes where the state of matter changes but the chemical composition stays the same. The molecular structure (H₂O) is identical in ice, water, and steam.
